chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The one for whom there is no wergild

text: إِذَا اطَّلَعَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى قَوْمٍ يُشْرِفُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ خَلَلِ شَيْ‏ءٍ لَهُمْ فَرَمَوْهُ فَأَصَابُوهُ فَقَتَلُوهُ أَوْ فَقَئُوا عَيْنَهُ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِمْ غُرْمٌ وَ قَالَ إِنَّ رَجُلًا اطَّلَعَ مِنْ خَلَلِ حُجْرَةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَجَاءَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) بِمِشْقَصٍ لِيَفْقَأَ عَيْنَهُ فَوَجَدَهُ قَدِ انْطَلَقَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَيْ خَبِيثُ أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ لَوْ ثَبَتَّ لِي لَفَقَأْتُ عَيْنَيْكَ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Al A’la Bin Al Fuzayl,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When a man emerges upon a people to overlook upon them, or look at them from a crack of something of them, so they pelt him and it hits him, so he is either killed or blinds his eye, so there is no penalty upon them’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘A man peeked at Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> from a crack of his<sup>saww</sup> chamber, so Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> came over with an arrow in order to poke his eyes, so he<sup>saww</sup> found him to have gone away. So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Where is the filthy one? But, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, had he remained for me<sup>saww</sup>, I<sup>saww</sup> would have poked his eyes’.
